user_query:
Create a comprehensive internal playbook for our team's new serverless workflow runner initiative. Read input/company_policies.json and input/scenarios.txt and integrate their contents. Then produce all of the following deliverables under the output/ directory:

1) output/playbook.md — A structured playbook grounded in serverless workflow runner concepts with these eight sections as top-level headings: Overview, Quickstart, Patterns, Debugging, Performance, Security, Migration, and Quick Reference. Requirements:
- Each section must include clear bullet lists and actionable steps tailored to our team.
- Incorporate our exact policy lines from input/company_policies.json where relevant (call out the company name and policies explicitly in the text).
- Address every scenario from input/scenarios.txt with concrete guidance in the appropriate sections (e.g., debugging for permission errors, performance for cold starts, migration for rollback plans).
- In Security, include controls for authentication/authorization, least privilege, data encryption, comprehensive logging, and incident response.
- Include guidance on idempotency and webhook signature verification for payment-related webhook flows, and note any SCA/3D Secure considerations for checkout flows handled by webhooks.
- In Migration, include a pre-migration checklist, an explicit rollback plan, and verification steps before and after cutover.

2) output/security_matrix.csv — A security and operations control matrix with header exactly: Domain,Practice,Presence_in_Serverless,Presence_in_Payments,Notes. Include at least these six rows: Authentication, Idempotency Keys, Webhook Signatures, Rate Limiting, Logging, Data Encryption. For Presence_in_Serverless and Presence_in_Payments, use only Yes/No/Partial.

3) output/summary.json — A machine-readable summary with keys: sections (array of the eight section titles used), policies_included (array of the exact policy lines you incorporated), word_count (integer word count of output/playbook.md), has_payments_comparison (boolean indicating whether you compared serverless runner controls with payment/webhook controls), and scenarios_addressed (array of the exact scenario lines from input/scenarios.txt you covered).

Use only relative workspace paths. Do not create any files outside output/.
